definition: SQL parser grammar-reduction edges (yy_reduce, yy_destructor) are reached via deep call chains; context-sensitive coverage treats each distinct parse-reduction call path as new coverage, incrementally building deeper parse trees and complex SQL structures that bare-edge naive saturates on from shallower paths.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook folds the SELECT-parse call chain (sqlite3RunParser -> sqlite3Parser -> yy_reduce) into the edge-map index, making the reducer edge fresh coverage that keeps SELECT-prefixed inputs in the corpus and lets naive_ctx extend the parse incrementally.
signature.one_line: context-indexed yy_reduce edge retains SELECT-prefixed inputs for deeper parse

analysis.summary_one_line: A syntactically valid SELECT statement with a dotted column-expression list that drives the yy_reduce reduction at 157646's true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that reaches the yy_reduce reducer at 157646 via the deep SELECT-parse call context (sqlite3RunParser -> sqlite3Parser -> yy_reduce), so the edge is newly interesting under context-indexed coverage.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx winner seeds begin with 'SELECT' (53 45 4c 45 43 54) and drive yy_reduce to hit the blocker true side 11 times with avg hitcount 140 on the branch, far above the loser's 3.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds resolve the branch in only 2/10 trials with avg hitcount 3, never sustaining the deep parse call path that would make the reducer edge a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage event.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the running caller-chain hash into the edge-map index, so the yy_reduce reducer edge reached through the SELECT-expression call chain registers as a new (call-context, edge) bucket; this keeps intermediate SELECT-shaped inputs in the corpus and lets naive_ctx incrementally extend the parse where bare-edge naive coverage sees nothing new.

evidence_trail.claim: The two decisive pairs differ by distinct techniques: value_profile and ctx_coverage.
The second decisive pair is the ctx_coverage delta (naive_ctx beats naive).
The blocked side is the true branch inside the yy_reduce parser reducer.
value_profile winner seeds begin with the literal bytes for 'SELECT '.
naive_ctx winner seeds also begin with the SELECT keyword.
Winner inputs all carry SELECT at offsets 0x00-0x05 while loser inputs carry non-SELECT bytes there.
Loser-blocking seeds do not begin with SELECT (e.g. start with 'T ').
Winner inputs drive expression resolution (resolveExprStep) that loser inputs never reach.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side 11 times while the loser never does.
The ctx_coverage winner shows a far larger branch hitcount than the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=140  loser=3

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile rewards inputs whose CMP operands move closer to matching via the CMP_MAP distance bucket.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx folds the call-context hash into the edge index so the same edge counts as new coverage on a new call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the blocker reduction can be reached by a non-SELECT input (no keyword/expression prefix dataflow) yet value_profile still resolves it significantly faster than naive — showing the win is not driven by CMP_MAP ratcheting toward the SELECT-expression prefix.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the divergence is driven by tokenizer/keyword CMP feedback specifically (CMP_MAP gradient) versus generic deep-parse difficulty is inferred from the SELECT-prefix byte pattern, not from a named CMP comparison in the source, since the blocker source text was not available.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook registers the LP-select-RP reduce action edge as a new (call-context, edge) bucket along the deep parser call chain, rewarding parenthesized nested-SELECT inputs that bare-edge naive saturates on from shallower paths and never retains.
signature.one_line: context bucket along deep parse chain rewards nested-SELECT LP-select-RP reduction

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a parenthesized subquery used as an expression (LP select RP) so the parser fires yy_reduce case 209.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: SQL text that parses to the grammar rule expr ::= LP select RP, i.e. a '(' opening a nested SELECT statement closed by ')' in an expression position, so yy_reduce dispatches the case-209 reduce action at line 157866.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds begin 'SELECT' and contain a '(' followed by a second nested 'SELE'/'SELECT' token (e.g. '(+++S...((((((SELECT'), giving the parser a parenthesized inner SELECT that reduces via LP select RP; the true side is taken 10 times under W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short fragments (15-79 bytes) with no parenthesized inner SELECT and never reach yy_reduce case 209 at all (the whole yy_reduce body shows T=0 throughout L), so the true side is taken 0 times.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so reaching the deep parser reduce path along the renameParseSql/sqlite3RunParser/sqlite3Parser/yy_reduce call chain registers as new (call-context, edge) coverage; this rewards the intermediate parser states that build a nested-SELECT expression, whereas bare-edge naive coverage saturates on the same edges seen via shallower paths and never retains the inputs that drive toward the LP select RP reduction.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side is the case-209 reduce action 'expr ::= LP select RP' inside yy_reduce.
Only the naive_ctx winner takes the true branch; naive loser blocks it across all trials.
At the blocker line the winner takes true 10 times while the loser never enters that side of the function.
Winner-resolving seeds contain a SELECT prefix with a '(' opening a nested SELECT token, satisfying the LP select RP structure.
Loser-blocking seeds are short fragments lacking any parenthesized inner SELECT.
The blocker is reached through a deep parser call chain ending at yy_reduce, the path that context coverage distinguishes.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: hop 2  sqlite3.c:sqlite3Parser  (/src/sqlite3/bld/sqlite3.c:158501-158664, calls sqlite3.c:yy_reduce at line 158536)

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx's distinguishing technique is folding the call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same edge counts as new coverage on a new call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx uses only havoc/token mutators with no I2S or CMP_MAP comparison-solving.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no
CMP_MAP.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on the LP-select-RP reduce path where baseline naive resolves the branch as fast as naive_ctx, showing the call-context-indexed coverage gives no advantage at reaching the nested-subquery reduce action.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte evidence shows a nested-SELECT/'(' pattern in winner seeds but does not directly prove the parser reduces exactly via rule 209 rather than some other parenthesized-expression rule reaching the same line.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook records yy_destructor edges at distinct (call-context, edge) slots per parse-reduction call path, so SELECT-driven deep reduction paths earn new coverage that bare-edge naive collapses to already-seen slots and never prioritizes.
signature.one_line: context buckets per parse-reduction path enable yy_destructor true branch via SELECT input

analysis.summary_one_line: Reaching yy_destructor's true side needs deep SQL parser reductions (SELECT-prefixed grammar) that exercise the destructor under a specific call context.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A syntactically rich SQL input (leading SELECT keyword followed by nested parser productions) that drives the Lemon parser through deep yy_reduce reductions, so yy_destructor is invoked from a destructor-bearing grammar symbol and takes the true branch at line 155710.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds begin with the literal bytes 'SELECT' (53 45 4c 45 43 54) and contain repeated '(' and '$' structure that drives many parser reductions; the blocker true side fires (L155710 T=8 in W) and yy_reduce executes 2680 false / hundreds of true reductions only in W, reaching the destructor.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds are short, malformed fragments (15-79 bytes) that do not begin with SELECT and never drive the parser into the deep reduction paths; the blocker true side is never taken (L155710 T=0 in L) and yy_reduce sees only 61 reductions.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same yy_destructor edge recorded under a new (call-context, edge) pair counts as new coverage; this rewards inputs that reach yy_destructor via novel parse-reduction call paths, which plain naive collapses into one already-seen edge and so never prioritizes. Both fuzzers share identical havoc/token mutators with no I2S or CMP_MAP, so the only enabling difference is coverage granularity.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx winning over naive with delta ctx_coverage.
naive_ctx resolves the branch in 9/10 trials while naive blocks in all 10.
The blocker is in the Lemon parser destructor function yy_destructor.
All winner-resolving seeds begin with the literal SELECT keyword bytes.
Winner bytes at offsets 0x00-0x05 are concentrated on SELECT while loser bytes are scattered fragments.
The loser-blocking seeds are short malformed fragments that do not start with SELECT.
The blocker true side is taken in W but not in L.
Deep parser reduction (yy_reduce) executes vastly more in W than L, evidencing deeper parse paths only in the winner.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds call context into the edge-map index so a new (call-context, edge) pair is new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge

evidence_trail.claim: Both involved fuzzers use the same havoc/token mutators with no I2S or CMP_MAP.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no
CMP_MAP.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which the destructor-bearing branch is reachable via only one fixed call context shows naive resolving it as fast as naive_ctx, indicating context granularity (not parse depth) was not the enabling factor.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The link from 'SELECT-prefixed grammar drives deep reductions' to the specific yymajor token that makes yy_destructor take its true side is inferred from call-chain counts, not from a confirmed grammar-symbol-to-branch mapping.
